name: Enable/Disable Rule
summary: ENABLE or DISABLE a field based on another field's value — field remains visible but read-only when disabled
whenToUse: Graying out a field until a prerequisite is completed
Making a 'Kind of death' field editable only when 'Is dead' is checked
When you want the field visible for context but not editable
Disabling a submit section until all required information is provided
notes: ENABLE: field starts disabled, becomes enabled when condition is TRUE
DISABLE: field starts enabled, becomes disabled when condition is TRUE — the INVERSE of ENABLE
Use ENABLE/DISABLE when you want the field to remain visible but toggled between editable and read-only
Disabled fields display their current value but don't accept input
Unlike SHOW/HIDE, the field is always visible — use this for fields that provide context
Choose ENABLE vs DISABLE based on the default state: use ENABLE when disabled-by-default, DISABLE when enabled-by-default
You can combine ENABLE/DISABLE with conditional required validation (if/then) to make a field required only when it is enabled
